100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 7, 1926, a significant earthquake struck the region of Central Italy, causing widespread damage and loss of life, prompting urgent rescue efforts. Meanwhile, in the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition intensified as various states grappled with enforcement challenges and public discontent. In international affairs, tensions continued to rise in the Far East, particularly concerning Japan's expansionist policies.
Updated: Sunday, June 7, 2026 at 08:11:00 AM CDT
